headline: Exclusive-Intel, AMD sign long-term server CPU deals with Chinese clients as prices surge, sources say
summary: U.S. chipmaking giants Intel and Advanced Micro Devices are signing longer-term purchase commitments with Chinese server customers for data-centre processors as prices ‌surge, two people familiar with the talks said.  The move highlights a broader consequence of the ‌AI boom: demand has spread beyond AI accelerators to memory, networking gear and server processors, giving suppliers greater leverage to seek long-term purchase ​deals.  AI data centres require not only Nvidia-style graphics processors (GPUs) but also large numbers of central processing units (CPUs) to support servers, storage, networking and inference workloads.

headline: Why Gartner Stock Swooned in the First Half of 2026
summary: It's hard to compete with increasingly sophisticated AI models.

content.title: Why Gartner Stock Swooned in the First Half of 2026
content.summary: It's hard to compete with increasingly sophisticated AI models.

thumbnail.caption: Trends in the IT information market aren't in favor of Gartner's (NYSE:IT) business, and the company's recent stock trajectory reflects this. Shares of the veteran IT data and analysis company lost nearly 49% of their value in the first six months of this year. Read on for my take on whether Gartner can turn this around .sdasdafasdfMore than anything, investors are rightfully concerned that artificial intelligence (AI) can drain business from Gartner. Increasingly these days, decision-makers in the tech field (and, let's be clear, in many sectors) are leaning on AI models to help them analyze markets and aid in strategizing. That's a clear threat to the foundational analysis business of the company. https://www.fool.com/investing/stock-market/market-sectors/information-technology/ai-stocks/ That fear grew in February, when Gartner released its final earnings report for 2025. Although the company handily beat analyst estimates and sounded a very bullish note on the fourth quarter and its looming future, its growth rates weren't grandly impressive. https://www.streetinsider.com/Investing/Gartner+shares+rise+as+Q4+earnings+trump+expectations/25933890.html Global contract value -- an important metric for the company -- inched less than 1% higher year over year (to $5.2 billion), filtering down into total revenue growth of barely over 2%, to $1.8 billion. https://www.wsj.com/market-data/quotes/IT/financials/annual/income-statement https://investor.gartner.com/static-files/02c9ecd4-3f2a-4a66-af49-4ff20645e8e3 slide 5Also, it proffered full-year guidance of almost $6.46 billion for revenue, which if achieved would be lower than 2025's almost $6.5 billion. More uncomfortably, its forecast was notably under the $6.52 billion analyst consensus.As the year progressed, it became more apparent that many IT departments in businesses were devoting more spend into direct AI implementations and enhancements. It follows that some pushed analysis and data services well down the priority ladder.Gartner's fourth quarter earnings came not long after the company announced it was divesting its digital market division, its business-to-business (B2B) unit. The buyer of the unit, which comprises the online platforms Capterra, Software Advice, and GetApp, was software B2B specialist G2, and the base price was eventually revealed to be $110 million.Gartner's stock took quite a hit after this announcement; while the division had seen declines in both revenue and margins, that price was considered something of a fire-sale figure. sdafsdasfdaGartner's subsequent first quarter 2026 earnings report did little to settle investor nerves about the state of its business. Contract value again limped only slightly higher, by 1% (to $5.3 billion)  but revenue reversed course during the period, slipping by 1.5% to $1.5 billion. The company also cut its top-line annual guidance to under $6.41 billion for the year. https://investor.gartner.com/static-files/05ed704c-f905-4c68-9b57-e55eed5df9eb slide 5On a brighter note, headline net income bumped 5% higher to $222 million, and guidance was raised for both earnings per share not under generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP) and free cash flow. The former was pushed up to $13.25 from $12.30, while the latter got a boost to $1.16 billion from nearly $1.14 billion.In order to rebound in this new half of the year, Gartner will probably need to do a better job showing investors that it can benefit from the relentless AI surge rather than being battered by it. Until it does so, it might be best not to be invested in the stock.

headline: AMD expected to launch next generation of AI infrastructure to challenge Nvidia
summary: Advanced Micro Devices is set to launch a raft of AI hardware that ‌will rival Nvidia on Thursday at an event at a downtown convention ‌center in San Francisco.  AMD is attempting to capture market share from Nvidia in the fast-growing data center ​chip sector, especially for so-called inference computing, which is the data crunching that occurs when a user queries a chatbot such as OpenAI's ChatGPT.  AMD is expected to show off the company's data center hardware that includes its first-generation server racks called Helios, which ‌it is marketing as a ⁠rival to a similar design from Nvidia, which is rolling out its second-generation product this year.

AMD is expected to show off the company's data center hardware that includes its first-generation server racks called Helios, which ‌it is marketing as a ⁠rival to a similar design from Nvidia, which is rolling out its second-generation product this year.
